programme.title: The Aviators S1 - "Can a Non-pilot Land an Airliner?"
programme.sub-title: Can a Non-pilot Land an Airliner?

programme.title: The Aviators S1 - "Air Traffic Control Tower"
programme.desc: An inside look at one of the most technologically advanced towers in the world.

programme.title: The Aviators S1 - "Rick Volker"
programme.desc: Sara interviews the air show performer during the off-season to talk about spring conditioning.

programme.title: The Aviators S1 - "En Route Control"
programme.desc: Most air traffic control does NOT take place in a tower but rather in an area control center like this.

programme.title: The Aviators S1 - "Aircraft Auctions"
programme.desc: Getting a bargain while purchasing aircraft in a unique and fast-paced way.

programme.title: Power & S2 - "Al Baum"
programme.desc: Al Baum likes to invent crazy machines that give a different perspective on mobility. They are not meant as practical mode of transportation, but they are fun.

programme.title: Health Explored S2 - "Adaptation"
programme.desc: Travel to La Paz, Bolivia, to see how high altitude impacts the body. From science to tradition, visit the witches market and learn about an ancient Inca medicine for altitude sickness

programme.title: Health Explored S2 - "Superfoods"
programme.desc: The high altitude environment makes foods that are considered "super". From quinoa, to special beans and salt - We travel from the Uyuni Salt flats back to La Paz to work with a Master Chef.

programme.title: Health Explored S2 - "Mother Earth"
programme.desc: Travel to the world's highest lake to see what is happening in this fragile ecosystem. We finish our Bolivian adventure with a ceremony honouring our planet.

programme.title: Health Explored S2 - "What We Learned"
programme.desc: The small team share their most impactful moments, stories, places and people, and how they changed their lives and health.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S6 - "Leftovers Again?"
programme.desc: Terry and Larry test things they never got to from other episodes. Pull up a chair and get ready for leftovers, and a surprise guest!

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S6 - "Punishing Larry"
programme.desc: Grenades, propane tanks, and a bathtub, all adds up to some big tests of the popular comic book character.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S6 - "Terry Loves Action Movies"
programme.desc: From Polar, to James Bond, the Lethal Weapon, Terry loves action movies and is putting some of the best ones to the test!

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S6 - "Terry Nobody"
programme.desc: We're putting the action-packed film Nobody to the test. Booby Traps, Claymores and Riot shields make for an interesting day at the range.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering S1 - "Stone Age Secrets"
programme.desc: Even before the start of recorded history, ancient engineers across the World were erecting giant monuments. What was their purpose?

programme.title: Ancient Engineering S1 - "The Taj Mahal And The Golden Age Of Islam"
programme.sub-title: The Taj Mahal And The Golden Age Of Islam
programme.desc: Awe-inspiring palaces are the legacy that demonstrates the brilliance of Islamic engineers, culminating arguably the most beautiful building in the World - the Taj Mahal.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering S2 - "Earliest Arenas: The Colosseum"
programme.desc: Since their earliest existence in ancient Greek and Roman civilizations, stadiums have been regarded as achitectural masterpieces and focal poins in their surrounding cities, but none more so than the Colosseum in Rome.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering S2 - "How Gold Changed The World"
programme.desc: Join us as we dig deep in this episode to uncover how gold was mined and transformed into everyday objects like jewellery and currency, and even to adorn holy temples and statues.

programme.title: Secrets of the Brain S2 - "Gut"
programme.desc: Jack meets a poker player who has won millions in high stakes international tournaments using his gut instincts.

programme.title: Secrets of the Brain S2 - "Twins"
programme.desc: Dr Jack Lewis is on a mission to understand why identical twins are such an asset to science from development in the womb onwards.

programme.title: Extraordinary Humans S1 - "The Beast Man / Hawk-Eyes"
programme.desc: The people Thom investigates in this episode claim to harness powers that it was thought existed only in the animal kingdom.

programme.title: Extraordinary Humans S1 - "Human Cat / Human Bat"
programme.desc: Thom meets a man who claims to be the fastest human on all fours and a man who uses a form of echo-location to see the world around him using sound.

programme.title: Extraordinary Humans S1 - "Human Camel / Mega Arm"
programme.desc: Thom tracks down a man who has a right arm bigger than his left and sets another man the ultimate challenge to extinguish are using the water in his stomach.

programme.title: Extraordinary Humans S1 - "Fearless / Iron Chest"
programme.desc: Thom tracks down two more extraordinary humans. One balances on the edges of the world and one attempt to withstand the weight of a crushed car on his chest.

programme.title: The Military Tech Show S1 - "Typhoon Fighter, Combat Medics, USS Dwight D. Eisenhower"
programme.sub-title: Typhoon Fighter, Combat Medics, USS Dwight D. Eisenhower
programme.desc: The Military Tech Show captures the moment when BAE System's radical new pilot helmet, the Striker II, goes operational for the first time in a Typhoon fighter aircraft; how interactive technology is helping train military combat medics.

programme.title: The Military Tech Show S1 - "Spear 3 Missile, Battlefield VR, Spy Drones"
programme.sub-title: Spear 3 Missile, Battlefield VR, Spy Drones
programme.desc: European manufacturer MBDA give a hands-on demonstration of their state-of-the-art missile, the Spear 3. Learn how scientists are using a virtual reality headset to see the battlefield in a revealing new way.

programme.title: The Military Tech Show S1 - "Radar Tech, Imperial War Museum, Land Mine Detection"
programme.sub-title: Radar Tech, Imperial War Museum, Land Mine Detection
programme.desc: We have rare access to the German Aerospace Center outside Munich where scientists are using new forms of radar for surveillance and detecting land mines.

programme.title: The Military Tech Show S1 - "RAF Station, Typhoon Squadron, The Flight Deck"
programme.sub-title: RAF Station, Typhoon Squadron, The Flight Deck
programme.desc: We're at Royal Air Force station Brize Norton, Oxfordshire, where we've been given an exclusive look at the RAF's ground-breaking, multi-role refuelling and transporter aircraft, the Voyager.

programme.title: Battle Factory S1 - "An F16, a Sword, and Simulated Ammunition"
programme.sub-title: An F16, a Sword, and Simulated Ammunition
programme.desc: Brainpower and firepower transform 8,000 kilograms of computer-controlled machinery into a legendary dogfighter with the best fight and flight record in the sky; the Lockheed Martin F16.

programme.title: Battle Factory S1 - "Camouflage Makeup, a Machine Gun, and an Amphibious Military ATV"
programme.sub-title: Camouflage Makeup, a Machine Gun, and an Amphibious Military ATV
programme.desc: Developed from solidified vegetable oil, this military grade compound is a long way from department store concealer: it’s sweat proof, infrared resistant, and stays on in the drenching downpour. For soldiers, especially Special Ops and snipers, camo paint completes their covert coverage.

programme.title: Battle Factory S1 - "Combat Boots, a Pistol, a UAV, and a Riflescope"
programme.sub-title: Combat Boots, a Pistol, a UAV, and a Riflescope
programme.desc: For the soldier, the army boot is more than protective footwear. From the parade ground to the battlefield, the boot is a symbol of discipline and determination that marches the soldier right through his military career.

programme.title: Combat Forces S1 - "82nd Airborne"
programme.desc: Paratroopers are elite troops, tougher and faster than foot soldiers. We join the 82nd Airborne training for high-risk, behind-enemy-lines missions.

programme.title: Combat Forces S1 - "Navy Divers"
programme.desc: Ship divers tackle dangerous missions at sea, from clearing nets and mines to rescuing lives. We explore their intense training and high-stakes work.

programme.title: Combat Forces S1 - "Combat Trauma Care"
programme.desc: Combat Casualty Care trains soldiers to save lives on the battlefield, ensuring rapid response to injuries from IEDs and other deadly threats.

programme.title: Secret Wars Uncovered S1 - "Coup In Crimea"
programme.desc: The Crimea Annexation in February 2014 was the biggest and most blatant land grab in Europe since WWII and shook the very foundations upon which peace in the region had been based ever since. How did Russia execute the annexation?
